pattern: A perfect binary tree lets you walk one level at a time using only the pointers you just finished setting, trading an explicit queue for the structure's own guaranteed shape.
hints: Recursion or a queue solves it in O(n) extra space, but the tree's guaranteed perfect shape means you never actually need to store a level.
Once a level's next pointers are wired, treat that level itself as a linked list and use it to reach every node of the level below.
Walk the current level via next; for each node link its two children together, and link its right child to the next node's left child.

edge_inputs.description: perfect tree of 7 nodes, three full levels
perfect tree of 3 nodes with mixed sign values
7-node tree mixing negative, zero and positive values
every node shares the same value, so a solution that matches nodes by value instead of by pointer identity cannot tell them apart
perfect 31-node tree at the value extremes across five full levels

stress.description: random perfect binary trees of depth 0 through 5
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    depth = rng.randint(0, max(0, min(5, size)))
    n = (1 << depth) - 1
    vals = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
